slide_1775714686996_wa9ai48xl.name: Slide 1
expressions: title_1=title(at(2.8,20.9),"Application of Derivatives",fc(tan),pd(6),border(black,radius(18)),fs(36),notes("In this problem, we explore an application of derivatives—finding maximum values in real life."))
write_4=desc(at(7,6.5),"text(A ball is thrown into the air from a building 60 m high, travels along a path given by) h(x)=60+x-(x^(2))/(60),",fs(20),t(0.5))
write_3=desc(at(9.2,6.4),"text(where) x text(is the horizontal distance from building and) h(x) text(is height of the ball).text(What is the max height the)",fs(20),t(0.5))
write_7=desc(at(21.5,16.5),"60m",t(0.5),notes("This is the building with a height of 60 meters."))
notes_2=notes("This curve represents the motion of the ball; additionally, its equation is")
write_9=desc(at(13.2,24.9),"text(Vertex)text(,)text(Max height)",fs(20),notes("The highest point on the curve, called the vertex, represents the maximum height of the ball."))
write_10=desc(at(20.4,29.4),"text(Max height)=?",fs(20),notes("So our goal is to find this maximum height."))

slide_1775718944565_vbuybe2go.name: Slide 2
expressions: title_1=title(at(3.7,7.5),"Solution",fc(lightpink),pd(6),border(black,radius(18)),fs(25),notes("Let’s now solve the problem step by step."))
write_1=write(at(7,7.5),"h(x)=-(1)/(60)x^(2)+x+60",fadein(0.5),fs(20),notes("First, we rewrite the given function in standard quadratic form"))
write_19=write(at(13.4,7.4),"text(The horizontal distance )x, text(at the vertex :) x=-(b)/(2a).",fs(20),fadein(0.5))
notes_2=notes("Substituting the values of a and b into the formula.")
write_13=write(at(17.3,26.6),"=30 text( metres)",fs(20),notes("So, the horizontal distance at the vertex is x = 30 meters."))
write_14=write(at(21.1,7.4),"text(To find the maximum height substitute )x=30 text( in original equation.) ",fs(20),fadein(0.5))
write_18=write(at(24.3,28.4),"=75m",fs(20),notes("Therefore, the maximum height reached by the ball is 75 meters."))

slide_1776076544337_c9ifzjlir.name: Slide 3
expressions: title_2=title(at(7,9.2),"1) Compare math(h(x)) with standard Quadratic Equation math(ax^2+bx+c=0), find math(a,b,c) values.",fs(22))
title_3=title(at(9.4,9.1),"2) The vertex of a parabola is the max height use vertex formula math( x=-b/ {2a} )",fs(22))
title_4=title(at(11.8,9.1),"3) To find the maximum height substitute the value of math(x) in math(h(x)).",fs(22))
